test_summary: 13 passed (10 unit + 3 integration); S14 browser verification: V1-V3 pass, V4 partial (env_data_missing, not a code defect)
notes: Analysis completed; findings written to two output files.

single_modal_partial.finding: The modal partial (dashboard/templates/fragments/batch_overlap_modal.html) is a single clean fragment. The <li> elements produced by {% for glob in section.globs %} are extension-ready for CR-00078's per-row Ignore controls. No structural rewrite forced. The data-iw-modal-root attribute on the outer backdrop div is the JS injection point for CR-00078 BatchOverlapIgnore swaps.

truncation_gap.finding: S14 browser verification confirmed 7 <li> elements rendered with no +N truncation (V2: all match tooltip). S05 integration tests assert the presence of glob strings by looping over section.globs, which implicitly covers truncation for the seeded data. However, no explicit assertion for the ABSENCE of +N pattern exists in the test suite. Recommendation: add assert '+N' not in response.text in the happy-path test, or assert <li> count matches sum(len(s.globs) for s in sections). This is a minor coverage gap; CR-00078's test suite should fill it.

f404_path_coverage.finding: S05 covers 404 via 2 tests: test_status_404_no_event (no DaemonEvent rows) and test_status_404_event_outside_window (event at now()-301s). S14 V4 also exercised 404 when seed events aged beyond the 300s window. Both code and browser paths are covered.

fix_cycle_cost.finding: Zero fix cycles across all steps (S01, S03, S05, S14). Each step completed on the first attempt. Root cause of success: (1) design doc was detailed enough to avoid ambiguity, (2) S01 stub was replaced wholesale rather than patched, (3) _overlap_window_cutoff() helper was extracted early, preventing duplication bugs. No workflow improvements needed.

scope_discipline.finding: All 7 changed files are within dashboard/ and tests/. No orch/, executor/, or other non-dashboard directories were modified. Scope discipline is clean.

carry_forward.finding: Modal partial's block hierarchy (iw-modal-backdrop → iw-modal-container → iw-modal-body → iw-modal-section → iw-modal-file-list → <li>) supports CR-00078 extension via CSS class targeting. CR-00078 can inject per-row Ignore controls into <li> elements and add a master 'Ignore all' button in the iw-modal-section without rewriting the outer structure. Proposed CR-00078 prep: add data-blocker-id and data-held-item-id data attributes to each <li> for ignore action query parameters.
carry_forward.cr00078_prep_finding: CR-00078 should add data-blocker-id and data-held-item-id attributes to each <li> element in the modal to support BatchOverlapIgnore query parameters, without modifying the endpoint. The iw-modal-file-list class and the data-iw-modal-root attribute on the backdrop div are the primary extension points.

overall_assessment.summary: CR-00077 implementation is correct, within scope, and designed for CR-00078 extension. The only minor finding is the absence of an explicit +N truncation test in S05, which S14 manually confirmed was not an issue. No blockers.
